# REGIME READ — 2026-07-17

## Changes Since Previous Read
- **Stage: 3 → 4 (regime-change) — the pre-committed downgrade. The prior read's Stage-4 re-entry triggers were named in advance and THREE of them fired at once.** This is not goalpost-moving after the fact: last read explicitly wrote *"Stage 4 re-entry: VXN re-spikes toward 27+, or a semiconductor-led NDX break, or an oil re-ignition."* All three landed in a single session.
- **THE DOMINANT FACT: last read's bullish turn was the one-session head-fake — the exact failure mode this sequence has been disciplined about, now realized in reverse.** VXN did NOT post a second sub-25 close; it **re-spiked to 27.34 (+6.59%)**, back above the 27 bearish trigger. The Jul-15 retrace to 25.65 is now confirmed as the artifact, not the +9.68% Jul-13 spike. **Every bullish leg failed; the DOWNTURN vol leg is re-armed and rising.**
- **The equity tape flipped from broadly green back to ALL-FIVE-DOWN, and the Nasdaq is leading the losses.** SPX −0.51%, Nasdaq Comp −1.47%, NDX −1.62%, DJI −0.20%, Russell −0.06% — **all five red, NDX worst.** The driver is a genuine **semiconductor/AI-valuation rout**: TSMC lifted 2026 capex guidance to $60–64B (from $52–56B), overshadowing a 77% earnings gain, and **Alphabet fell >4% on a Bloomberg report that Gemini 3.5 Pro is behind schedule** ([Yahoo Finance, Jul 16, 2026](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/live/stock-market-today-thursday-july-16-dow-sp-500-nasdaq-103116735.html); [TheStreet, Jul 16](https://www.thestreet.com/stock-market-today/stock-market-today-dow-jones-sp-500-nasdaq-updates-july-16-2026)).
- **THE ROUT IS ACCELERATING, NOT STABILIZING — and TQQQ has broken its floor.** Live IBKR read: **TQQQ ~$67.21, −4.99% intraday (Jul 17), prior close $70.74 (Jul 16, ≈−4.9%)** — **two consecutive ~5% down days**, and the second one has **broken below both the 200DMA ($68.09) and the 4-wk low ($68.08).** Nasdaq futures were **−2% overnight** as the global chip sell-off continued ([CNBC, Jul 16](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/16/stock-market-today-live-updates.html)).
- **The oil-hedge complex the prior read buried has re-ignited on Gulf tension, and trade-policy risk is back.** **Brent 85.92, +2.01%** (a genuine fourth impulse, near the 86 reclaim), **gold +0.29%** re-engaging — the oil↑+gold↑ risk-hedge pair is re-forming. Meanwhile the White House **announced 25% tariffs on Brazil and floated tariffs on 60 countries** ([Schwab market update](https://www.schwab.com/learn/story/stock-market-update-open); [Tax Foundation tariff tracker](https://taxfoundation.org/research/all/federal/trump-tariffs-trade-war/)).
- **Total score: +2 → −4 (delta −6).** The mirror image of last read, and symmetrically earned: the four lenses that each lifted a notch last read (Dealer, Macro, Breadth, Cross-asset) all reversed back down, and Geopolitical gave back its +1. The swing is not arithmetic over-correction — it is four-to-five independent deteriorations landing together, on the Nasdaq-specific data that prices TQQQ.
- **Triggers from previous read:**
  - **Bullish — second VXN close below 25:** **FAILED / CLEARED.** VXN went the opposite way to 27.34 (+6.59%).
  - **Bullish — VIX reclaim sub-15:** **FAILED.** VIX rose to 18.13 (+8.37%).
  - **Bullish — NDX joins tape green:** **FAILED.** NDX −1.62%, the worst index.
  - **Bullish — TQQQ 50DMA reclaim:** **FAILED.** TQQQ fell through the 200DMA and 4-wk low instead.
  - **Bearish — VXN re-closes above 27:** **FIRED.** 27.34.
  - **Bearish — VIX back above 18:** **FIRED.** 18.13.
  - **Bearish — semiconductor-led NDX break to −5% vs 52W high:** **FIRED.** NDX −5.6% vs 52W high, semis (TSMC/Alphabet) leading.
  - **Bearish — Brent reclaims 86 / real fourth impulse:** **HALF-FIRED.** 85.92, +2.01% — a real impulse; 86 reclaim is ~$0.08 away.
  - **Bearish — named geopolitical event:** **PARTIAL-FIRED.** "Gulf tension rose" lifting oil (not a fully named escalation, but a fresh geopolitical flare) + Trump tariff escalation.
  - **Bearish — HY OAS through 300 bp:** **STILL UNMEASURED. Week 11.** The one credit trigger remains blind.
- **Notable lens flips: four lenses reversed down, one gave back its bull tilt.** Dealer +1 → **−1** (both vol triggers fired); Macro 0 → **−1** (yields resume up + tariff escalation); Breadth 0 → **−1** (all-red, NDX −5.6%); Cross-asset 0 → **−1** (oil/gold hedge re-igniting on Gulf tension); Geopolitical +1 → **0** (Gulf flare + tariffs). Sentiment held **0** (risk-off but no capitulation signature — take-the-lower, symmetric with last read); Positioning held **0** (data gap, Week 11).

> **Snapshot mix (~1-session spread):** equities + **VXN** + 10Y = **Jul 16 close**; VIX + DXY + Brent + Gold = **Jul 17 intraday.** This read the spread *reinforces* the bear case cleanly: the confirming Nasdaq/equity/VXN deterioration is the Jul-16 close, and the fresher Jul-17 gauges (VIX +8.37% to 18.13, Brent +2.01%, the live TQQQ −4.99% continuation) all point the same way. Nothing in the spread cuts against the downgrade.
>
> **Headline #1 — the head-fake resolved bearish.** For two reads the picture hung on VXN. Last read it retraced and the tape went green; this read VXN re-spiked above 27, the tape went all-red, and the Nasdaq led down. The relief was the artifact.
>
> **Headline #2 — this is an AI-valuation event, and it is in the right index.** NDX is the worst major because the sell-off is *specifically* semis + AI infrastructure: TSMC's capex hike and the Gemini-3.5-Pro delay have investors "questioning whether massive AI investments can justify lofty valuations" ([CNBC](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/16/stock-market-today-live-updates.html)). For a 3×-Nasdaq instrument, the damage is concentrated exactly where the exposure is.
>
> **Headline #3 — TQQQ broke its floor and the decline is accelerating.** Two straight ~5% down days; the live Jul-17 print (~$67.21) is **below both the 200DMA and the 4-wk low.** This is a falling knife, not a stabilizing dip — the distinction that governs the put-selling read below.
>
> **Headline #4 — the macro/geopolitical backstop weakened at the same time.** Yields resumed up, Gulf tension re-lit oil (Brent +2%, near the 86 reclaim), gold re-engaged, and the White House re-escalated trade policy (25% Brazil, 60 countries floated). Four independent supports eroded in one session.

## Regime Stage Tracker
- **Current Stage: 4 — Regime-change (risk-off underway).** The prior read pre-registered VXN>27, a semis-led NDX break, and an oil re-ignition as the Stage-4 triggers; all three fired simultaneously, so the transition is the pre-committed call, not hindsight. **Held short of Stage 5 (risk-off bearish, VIX>20 sustained + credit widening) because VIX at 18.13 has not yet cleared 20 and there is no capitulation signature (F&G/credit unmeasured).** The character is regime-change: vol rising through 18 toward 20, breadth deteriorating sharply, the Nasdaq leg in a confirmed drawdown.
- **Stage Anchor:** Jul 16, 2026 — the VXN re-spike above 27 + the semis-led all-red tape (TSMC capex / Alphabet Gemini delay); this supersedes the Jul-15 green-relief session as the governing structural event.
- **Position:** Early Stage 4, one-to-two sessions in, still accelerating. **Stage-4 grind (50%):** VXN holds 27–30, VIX chops 18–22, semis keep leading the Nasdaq lower without a broad-index capitulation. **Stage-5 escalation (25%):** VIX clears 20 sustained, TQQQ presses toward the 13-wk low (~$57), and/or Brent reclaims 86 with Gulf tension naming a specific escalation. **Stage-3 re-stabilization (25%):** VXN falls back through 25 on a semis bounce, VIX reclaims sub-16, TQQQ reclaims the 200DMA/$68 — the mirror of the trigger set that just failed.
- **Confidence: Medium-High.** Why this high on a downgrade: the triggers were pre-registered and multiple fired together; the deterioration is corroborated across Dealer, Breadth, Cross-asset and Macro; the instrument itself confirmed with a two-day support break; and — unlike prior reads — the stress is measured on Nasdaq-specific data. **Not High because:** (1) VIX at 18.13 is not yet >20 — Stage 5 is unconfirmed; (2) **HY OAS unmeasured Week 11** — the contained-derating-vs-broad-credit-event discriminator is still blind, so whether this AI rout stays an equity-multiple event or spreads to credit is unanswerable; (3) breadth *internals* (A/D, McClellan, %>50DMA) still unretrievable — the all-red read is index-level; (4) no capitulation signature yet (VIX 18, no F&G), so a semis-bounce snapback remains live.
- **Bullish trigger (→ Stage 3):** **VXN re-closes below 25** AND **VIX reclaims sub-16** AND **TQQQ reclaims the 200DMA/$68** AND **semis stabilize (NDX green day).**
- **Bearish trigger (→ Stage 5):** **VIX clears 20 and holds** OR **VXN toward 30** OR **TQQQ presses the 13-wk low (~$57)** OR **Brent reclaims 86 with a named Gulf escalation** OR **HY OAS confirmed through 300 bp (unmeasured Week 11).**

## Regime Verdict
US risk environment **downgrades to Stage 4 (regime-change) — Medium-High confidence.** Last read's bullish turn was the one-session head-fake this sequence kept warning about: **VXN re-spiked to 27.34 (+6.59%), the tape went all-five-down with NDX leading (−1.62%, −5.6% vs 52W high) on a genuine semiconductor/AI-valuation rout** (TSMC capex hike + Alphabet's Gemini-3.5-Pro delay), and **the instrument confirmed — TQQQ posted two straight ~5% down days and the live Jul-17 print (~$67.21) broke below both its 200DMA and 4-wk low.** Three pre-registered Stage-4 triggers fired at once (VXN>27, VIX>18, semis-led NDX break), the oil-hedge complex re-ignited on Gulf tension (Brent +2.01%, gold +0.29%), and trade-policy risk returned (25% Brazil, 60 countries floated). Four lenses reverse a notch and the total swings +2 → **−4**. It holds *Stage 4* rather than Stage 5 only because VIX (18.13) has not cleared 20, there is no capitulation signature, and credit is unmeasured for an eleventh week. **The deterioration is real, corroborated across Dealer/Breadth/Cross-asset/Macro, and — like last read's improvement — measured on the Nasdaq-specific data that actually prices TQQQ.**

Both pre-registered vol triggers fired together and TQQQ IV is surging. But **no capitulation signature** (VIX 18, not 30+; no washout volume spike reported) and **F&G stale — Week 11.** Held 0 per take-the-lower, symmetric with last read: one-to-two down sessions + no survey data is insufficient to score −1 without a capitulation tell, just as one green session was insufficient to score +1 |

Neutral: Positioning, Sentiment, Geopolitical = 0. **Net −4, down from the prior read's +2 (delta −6).** The swing is the mirror image of last read — the same four lenses that each lifted +1 now drop, plus Geopolitical giving back its +1. **A VIX clear of 20 or a TQQQ press toward the 13-wk low drops this toward −6 / Stage 5; a VXN close back below 25 with a semis bounce lifts it back toward −1 / Stage 3.** |

## Diverging Signals
- **THE GOVERNING TENSION — a concentrated AI/semis derating vs no broad-market capitulation.** NDX is down 1.6% and TQQQ has broken its floor, but DJI (−0.20%) and Russell (−0.06%) barely moved and VIX is only 18. **This is (so far) a tech-multiple event, not a systemic risk-off.** Which way it resolves is the whole question: if it stays contained to AI valuations, this is a Stage-4 tech correction; if credit and breadth follow, it is the front edge of Stage 5. **The one gauge that would discriminate — HY OAS — is unmeasured for an eleventh week.**
- **The instrument is more bearish than the index.** NDX −1.62% but **TQQQ −4.99%** — 3× leverage plus a support break (below 200DMA and 4-wk low) means the thing actually traded is in a confirmed downtrend even though the broad tape is only mildly red. For a put-seller this asymmetry matters more than the index read.
- **Vol is spiking but not panicking.** VXN +6.59% and VIX +8.37% are real re-arming moves, yet both remain in the "elevated, not crisis" band (VXN 27, VIX 18). Premium is genuinely richer, but the level does not yet signal a washout — the tension between *fatter premium* and *falling knife* is unresolved.
- **This sequence over-read single-session moves in BOTH directions — apply the discipline symmetrically.** The Jul-15 green retrace was one session and it reversed; the Jul-16/17 rout is now two sessions and accelerating, which is *stronger* evidence — but a semis bounce could still snap it back. The Stage-4 call is made because multiple pre-registered triggers fired, not because two red days extrapolate to a crash.
- **Oil deflated without a named catalyst and has now re-inflated without one either.** Brent's fade (three impulses → four flat) and its re-ignition (+2.01% on "Gulf tension") both lack a retrievable specific driver — so its move is scored as a risk-off signal but the geopolitical lens is held at 0, not −1, pending a named escalation.

## TQQQ Read
- **Momentum:** RSI(14) **est. ~35–40 — approaching oversold, momentum firmly down.** Searchable values (49.9–69) are pre-rout and stale; after two ~5% down days the true read is falling fast. **Critically, this is oversold-and-still-falling, not oversold-and-stabilizing** — no mean-reversion tailwind yet, and the down-streak/support-break argue the knife is still moving.
- **Market structure:** **DOWNTURN — confirmed on both legs for the first time in this sequence.** Spot ~$67.21 is **−23.7% from the 4-wk high** ($88.09; DOWNTURN threshold is −20% / $70.47, now breached) **AND VXN 27.34 ≥ 25.** Both legs satisfied. Spot has also broken below the 200DMA ($68.09) and 4-wk low ($68.08). RALLY is irrelevant (~+31% away). (Caveat: the Jun-3/Jun-10 4-wk extremes are aging, but the DOWNTURN classification holds even against the tighter recent ~$67–$76 range.)
- **Trend:** **Below both moving averages — ~12% under the 50DMA ($76.39) and ~1.3% under the 200DMA ($68.09), which it broke intraday Jul 17.** The 200DMA break is the cleanest instrument-level Stage-4 confirmation; a reclaim of $68 is the first thing that would argue stabilization.
- **Volatility & premium environment:** **VXN 27.34 — re-spiked.** Est. TQQQ IV **~82%** (IBKR realized vol ~87% corroborates elevated actual movement), EM **±11.6%.** Premium is genuinely richer and *building*, not deflating — but it is building *because the underlying is in free-fall*. The vol richness that makes premium attractive is inseparable here from the directional risk producing it. Descriptive only.
- **Put-selling context: HOSTILE — the least favourable read of the sequence.** Turned HOSTILE (from last read's CAUTIOUS) because: (1) **TQQQ is a falling knife** — two consecutive ~5% down days with a **fresh break below the 200DMA and 4-wk low**, momentum down, not stabilizing; (2) **DOWNTURN structure is confirmed on both legs** (−23.7% from the 4-wk high, VXN ≥ 25); (3) both vol triggers fired (VXN>27, VIX>18) and the one-sigma path points down toward the 13-wk low (~$57); (4) the catalyst is a genuine AI/semis derating that is *specific to the Nasdaq TQQQ is levered to*; (5) **HY OAS unmeasured Week 11** — no read on whether the derating is spreading to credit. The one thing working *for* a premium-seller — richer IV (~82%) — is a direct product of the very drawdown that makes fresh downside exposure dangerous. **Net: HOSTILE — DOWNTURN confirmed, floor broken, vol re-armed, and the decline still accelerating; the richer premium is the knife's edge, not a tailwind. Context only — no strikes, sizes, expiries, or trade calls.**

## Forward Levels & Triggers
- **Bullish trigger (→ Stage 3 / stabilization):** **VXN re-closes below 25** AND **VIX reclaims sub-16** AND **TQQQ reclaims the 200DMA/$68** with a **semis bounce (NDX green day).**
- **Bearish trigger (→ Stage 5):** **VIX clears 20 and holds** OR **VXN toward 30** OR **TQQQ presses the 13-wk low (~$57)** OR **Brent closes above 86 with a named Gulf escalation** OR **HY OAS confirmed through 300 bp (unmeasured Week 11).**
- **Neutralizer:** the new pivot is **whether the AI/semis derating stays contained.** A semis stabilization + VXN back through 25 + a TQQQ 200DMA reclaim flips the read back toward Stage 3. Conversely, VIX clearing 20 or a TQQQ break toward $57 confirms Stage 5. The FOMC (Jul 28–29) sits just beyond the window as the next scheduled macro pivot.

## This Period's Catalysts
- **Jul 17, 2026 (Fri):** **Industrial Production & Capacity Utilization** ([Fed/NY Fed calendar](https://www.newyorkfed.org/research/calendars/nationalecon_cal)) — secondary, not a decision event.
- **~Jul 20–23, 2026:** **Netflix + first-wave tech earnings** — single-name/AI-sentiment sensitive given the current derating, but no QQQ-heavyweight (Mag-7) report confirmed inside the next 5 sessions.
- **Jul 28–29, 2026:** **FOMC decision** (press conference Jul 29, 2:00 pm ET) — the next critical decision event, ~7 trading sessions out, OUTSIDE the current window. Fed enters "with fairly positive numbers" (disinflation + firm retail sales) per [Schwab](https://www.schwab.com/learn/story/stock-market-update-open).
- **Jul 30, 2026:** Q2 GDP Advance, 8:30 am ET.
- **Week of Jul 27–31:** **Mega-cap (Mag-7) earnings cluster** — the next concentration-risk catalyst for NDX/TQQQ, directly into the AI-valuation debate now driving the tape.
- **Ongoing, unscheduled:** whether the semiconductor/AI derating is contained or spreading; whether VIX clears 20; whether Brent closes above 86 on Gulf tension; whether the Brazil/60-country tariff threat is enacted.

## Expiry-Window Flag
- **Catalysts inside the next 5 trading days (Jul 17, 20, 21, 22, 23):** **none critical.** June CPI already released (Jul 14); **FOMC is Jul 28–29 — OUTSIDE the window.** Netflix/first-wave tech earnings begin ~Jul 20 with no Mag-7 report confirmed inside the window.
